{"society_power": 7.5, "state_power": 7.0, "society_change": 0.5, "state_change": 0.5, "key_event": "Republican consolidation: Ferry Laws, press/union freedoms", "reasoning": "The early Third Republic simultaneously expanded state capacity (mass secular education, secularization, colonial reach, entrenched bureaucracy) and civic power (press and assembly laws, legalized unions, Commune amnesty, robust parliamentary contestation), pushing France firmly into the corridor; the Boulanger and Wilson scandals showed real fragility but did not reverse the twin gains."}
